title: OnePlus Nord 6 tipped to launch in India soon: Expected price, specs and everything expected
snippet: OnePlus could soon be launching the Nord 6 in India. The phone could debut with a Snapdragon 8s Gen 4 processor, 12GB RAM, and a 6.78-inch OLED display along with a 9,000mAh battery&nbsp;

title: Instagram is dropping end-to-end encryption soon: Here are 5 secure messaging apps you can use instead
snippet: Meta will end end-to-end encryption on Instagram starting May 8, 2026. The company says users can still use WhatsApp for encrypted messaging while affected users will receive instructions to download their data before the feature is removed.

title: Days after sweeping layoffs, Elon Musk vows xAI will match rivals Google, Anthropic and OpenAI this year

title: Techie shrinks dog's tumor by half after using ChatGPT to design ‘first personalized cancer vaccine’
snippet: Techie Paul Conyngham developed a custom mRNA vaccine for his dog, Rose, using ChatGPT and AlphaFold. The AI-assisted process involved genomic sequencing, leading to a significant reduction in her tumor size, illustrating AI's transformative role in veterinary medicine.

title: Will AI change how Computer Science is taught? Perplexity CEO thinks so
snippet: Perplexity’s Aravind Srinivas believes the AI era is pushing computer science back toward mathematics, logical thinking

title: What if thousands of AIs could predict the next market crash? MiroFish is trying to find out
snippet: MiroFish is a new open-source AI project that is gaining popularity on GitHub. It utilizes multi-agent technology to create a prediction engine that mimics real-world interactions

title: 'Reflexively reaching for AI': Shopify CEO reveals how he used Claude AI to replace traditional Windows software
snippet: Shopify CEO Tobi Lütke recently revealed how he used AI to create a web-based viewer for his annual MRI scans stored on a USB stick, avoiding the need for commercial Windows software

title: Elon Musk says Tesla’s ‘Terafab’ AI chip manufacturing project to launch next week
snippet: With the Terafab initiative, Tesla could eventually transform itself into an integrated device manufacturer (IDM) — designing and producing its own semiconductors.

title: India's NavIC satellite system faces challenge as IRNSS-1F failed after atomic clock malfunction: what we know
snippet: India's NavIC GPS alternative faces challenges after satellite IRNSS-1F failed, reducing operational satellites to three. A minimum of four is needed for complete coverage. The Union government last year stated that only four of the 11 satellites deployed for the NavIC system were fully operational.

title: xAI hiring reset: Elon Musk apologises to AI talent overlooked during early hiring, promises overhaul
snippet: Elon Musk said he will restructure xAI to improve talent acquisition, acknowledging past hiring mistakes.

title: Trump accuses Iran of using AI to spread disinformation

title: Peter Thiel's secretive Rome conference draws Church attention
snippet: The invitation-only conference, which runs until Wednesday, is not open to the press and its venue has not been publicly disclosed. Organisers quoted in the media say participants are drawn from academia, technology and religious circles.

snippet: Databricks is ramping up hiring and investment in India, expanding its Bengaluru R&D centre as enterprise demand grows. title: KC Ang quits as head of Tata Semiconductor Manufacturing
snippet: KC Ang has resigned as president and head of Tata Semiconductor Manufacturing, citing personal reasons. A veteran with decades in the chip industry, Ang had led Tata Electronics’ foundry operations. The company says it has a strong leadership team to continue its major semiconductor projects.

title: Most India GCCs still in early stages of DPDP compliance despite ticking 14-month deadline: Experts
